name: B-Epsilon Tree
overview: TrustDB's core data structure is a write-optimized B-epsilon tree with sorted BTreeMap internal buffers. Unlike traditional B-trees (optimized for reads) or LSM trees (optimized for writes but with high compaction overhead), the B-epsilon tree provides a tunable balance between read and write performance. Adaptive Epsilon Tuning (AET) adjusts the epsilon parameter at runtime based on workload characteristics.

howItWorks.title: Buffered Writes
howItWorks.description: Writes are buffered in internal nodes using sorted BTreeMap structures (not slot-based append-only, which avoids VMware patent claims). Buffers amortize disk I/O by batching multiple writes before flushing to children.

howItWorks.title: Epsilon Parameter
howItWorks.description: The epsilon parameter (0 < ε < 1) controls the buffer-to-fanout ratio. Higher epsilon = more buffer space = better write performance. Lower epsilon = more children = better read performance. Default: 0.8 (write-optimized).

howItWorks.title: Flush with Governance
howItWorks.description: When buffers exceed flush_threshold, messages cascade to children. During flush, CABM applies temporal decay, MPCI merges confidence from multiple parents, and WAT records write amplification telemetry.

howItWorks.title: Adaptive Tuning (AET)
howItWorks.description: AET monitors read/write ratios and adjusts epsilon at runtime. Write-heavy workloads get higher epsilon; read-heavy workloads get lower epsilon. Optimal measured: threshold=8192, epsilon=0.8 at 8,616 ops/s.

comparisons.narrative: The B-epsilon tree occupies a sweet spot between B-trees and LSM trees. It provides better write amplification than LSM (3.04 vs 10-30x) with competitive read performance, and better write throughput than B-trees while maintaining sorted order.

competitors.limitation: Excellent reads, but write amplification limited by in-place updates

competitors.approach: Log-Structured Merge tree with level compaction
competitors.limitation: Excellent writes, but WAF 10-30x and read amplification from multiple levels
WAF 1x but fragmentation over time; limited to single-writer
Discontinued by Percona; not embedded; no governance integration
